2605.19806.title: Chunking German Legal Code
2605.19806.paper_summary: This paper investigates chunking strategies for retrieval-augmented generation on German statutory law, using the German Civil Code as a structured benchmark corpus. We implement and compare a range of segmentation approaches, including structural units (sections, subsections, sentences, propositions), fixed-size windows, contextual chunking, semantic clustering, Lumber-style chunking, and RAPTOR-based hierarchical retrieval. All methods are evaluated on a legal question-answering dataset with section-level gold labels, measuring recall, query latency, index build time, and storage requirements. Results show that chunking strategies aligned with the inherent legal structure - particularly section and subsection - based retrieval-achieve the highest recall, while more complex approaches that override this structure perform worse. These simpler methods also offer favorable computational efficiency compared to LLM-intensive techniques such as contextual chunking, RAPTOR, and Lumber. The findings highlight a key trade-off between semantic enrichment and operational cost, and demonstrate that preserving domain-specific structure is critical for effective legal information retrieval.
2605.19806.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文研究了針對德國法定法律的檢索增強生成的分塊策略，使用德國民法典作為結構化基準語料庫。<lb/>我們實施並比較了一系列的分段方法，包括結構單元（章節、小節、句子、命題）、固定大小的窗口、上下文分塊、語義聚類、Lumber風格的分塊以及基於RAPTOR的層次檢索。<lb/>所有方法都在具有章節級金標籤的法律問答數據集上進行評估，測量回憶率、查詢延遲、索引構建時間和存儲需求。<lb/>結果顯示，與固有法律結構（特別是章節和小節）對齊的分塊策略在檢索中達到最高的回憶率，而覆蓋這一結構的更複雜方法表現較差。<lb/>這些更簡單的方法在計算效率上也相較於LLM密集型技術（如上下文分塊、RAPTOR和Lumber）提供了更有利的效率。<lb/>研究結果突顯了語義豐富性與運營成本之間的關鍵權衡，並展示了保留特定領域結構對於有效法律信息檢索的重要性。
2605.19806.author: Max Prior et.al.
2605.19806.authors: Max Prior, Natalia Milanova, Andreas Schultz
